frameworks.name: deep reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: RL trains a social planner to allocate resources in a multiplayer trust game.

frameworks.name: iterated multiplayer trust game
frameworks.one_line: Economic paradigm framing the common-pool resource social dilemma.

frameworks.name: conditional cooperation
frameworks.one_line: Baseline allocation heuristic against which the RL policy is benchmarked.

frameworks.name: neural network models of human players
frameworks.one_line: Networks imitate human behaviour to form a simulated economy for planner training.

core_question: Can a deep RL social planner design an allocation mechanism that sustains human contributions to a common-pool resource?
key_assumptions: Human player behaviour can be approximated by trained neural networks
Aggregate human surplus is an appropriate planner objective
Trust-game dynamics generalise the common-pool resource dilemma
manipulations: Allocation mechanism (RL planner vs unrestricted welfare vs conditional cooperation)
Available resource level conditioning the planner's generosity
Sanctioning of defectors via reduced allocations
key_findings: RL planner discovered a redistributive policy yielding large surplus and roughly equal gains
RL mechanism increased human surplus above unrestricted welfare and conditional cooperation baselines
Policy conditioned generosity on resources and temporarily sanctioned defectors
An explainable derived mechanism matched performance and was more popular with players
interpretations: Endogenously learned redistributive allocation can promote sustainable cooperation in social dilemmas
Examining RL policies can yield interpretable, human-acceptable governance mechanisms
